posts.title: There Is a Planet Where It Rains Glass Sideways

pinterest.title: There Is a Planet Where It Rains Glass Sideways
pinterest.description: HD 189733b, located 63 light-years away, has winds exceeding 8,700 kilometers per hour that blow molten silicate glass horizontally. The planet appears deep blue, not from water, but from the light-scattering properties of the glass particles in its atmosphere. https://surfaced-x.pages.dev/discoveries/there-is-a-planet-where-it-rains-glass-sideways #surfaced #discoveries

posts.title: The Shortest War in History Lasted 38 Minutes

pinterest.title: The Shortest War in History Lasted 38 Minutes
pinterest.description: The Anglo-Zanzibar War of 1896 lasted between 38 and 45 minutes. posts.title: Tardigrades: Microscopic "Water Bears" That Can Survive in Space

pinterest.title: Tardigrades: Microscopic "Water Bears" That Can Survive in Space
pinterest.description: Tardigrades, also known as water bears or moss piglets, are microscopic invertebrates renowned for their extreme resilience. These tiny creatures can survive conditions that would be lethal to almost any other form of life, including extreme radiation, vacuum of space, intense pressure, and temperatures from near absolute zero to over 150°C. posts.title: Ancient Roman Concrete Endures Millennia, Outperforming Modern Mixes

pinterest.title: Ancient Roman Concrete Endures Millennia, Outperforming Modern Mixes
pinterest.description: Roman concrete, used in structures like the Pantheon and marine piers, has remarkable durability, often lasting thousands of years.
